title: release: prepare for release `v1.27.0`
body:
Prepare for release `v1.27.0`

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]
[NO TESTS NEEDED]

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *flouthoc* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@nalind @TomSweeneyRedHat @rhatdan PTAL once approved and merged will cut a tag after this.
What are we planning to vendor into Podman v4.2?  I'm fine if this is headed to upstream/Fedora, but for Podman v4.2 we should be updating the v1.26 branch at this point.
I am fine with that also.  Back port it.
Lets just update the prior release and leave this in main.

> I am fine with that also. Back port it.

To be clear, backport what?

Oh i see but which backports are we looking for in `1.26.x` for info we recently released `1.26.2` for a BZ fix and after that we only have one unreleased version where `c/storage` is bumped: https://github.com/containers/buildah/commits/release-1.26

Edit: nvm I think we were looking for https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4155

In the 1.26 branch, I think it should have #4155 which is in progress on that branch.  After that merges, we should bump the Z version in the 1.26 branch.  Unless there are some commits that you think must get in.  There's just a lot of changes in there to add a week after feature freeze.
Containers/common and bug fixes back ported to current release.  I don't think there has been a lot of testing of this buildah release anyways.

> Containers/common and bug fixes back ported to current release.

#4155 currently pulls in containers/common v0.49.1 and cherry-picks https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4099, https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4071, and a small part of https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4048 to make the linters happy.  Which other bug fixes, specifically, do you have in mind?

I'd vote:  
#4135 stage_executor, output: generate correct artifacts for multi-stage builds and cached builds with --output
#4013 squash, layers: never use build cache when computing last step of last stage 
#4086 add: fail on bad HTTP response instead of writing to container for URL sources
#3998 run: allow re-using secret artifact from host in new RUN steps

At least those were the ones that were listed as fixes that I'm not seeing in 1.26.

As far as the testing goes, QE has actually run several test runs already, both against Buildah and then also via Podman.
Is possible to cherrypick also https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4064 ? Thanks

@lukasmrtvy Oh so sorry but `v1.26.3` is already out and since it was a minor it only included fixes and dependency bumps, discussion for `v1.27.0` is going here https://github.com/containers/buildah/discussions/4161
CI failure is issue with upstream and is issue with all the PR's,, being discussed here: https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4170#issuecomment-1207905624
Never mind, I missed that https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/198b4c360b84725fd13c81fc2984fb6b73da12aa removed the need for buildah.spec So long, old friend.
LGTM
@nalind PTAL

And I will note for the record that after we decided to use 1.26.* for Podman v4.2, we found that Podman 1.27 had been being used in Podman v4.2 already, although, at first glance, Podman looked to be using 1.26.4-dev. 

Long story short, we are preparing v1.27.0 for Podman v4.2 vendoring.
LGTM
@TomSweeneyRedHat @nalind @rhatdan one `/lgtm` is also needed to get this in.
/lgtm
